recent_review_text:
good for a short stay.  room gets warm from the sun but the air conditioning helps.

short walkable distance to roma termini.

one of the very few places to have a king size bed. 

the chair and sink are terrible. the chair is trying to be too artistic but takes up too much room. the sink is not practical, the tap is at the corner of the sink causing the water to splash everywhere meaning you can’t place things around the sink area as everything there would get wet.

the room is customizable with various hues such as red, green and blue. it’s an unnecessary artistic gimmick which may be a few seconds of intrigue that’s unnecessary.

with all that said, other than the weird chair that takes up too much room, the weird sink, it actually isn’t too bad. breakfast is reasonably priced, and just downstairs making it incredibly convenient. would recommend overall

recent_review_text: small but clean and friendly. the room was a little on the small side but very clean.  it had air conditioning which was a huge plus, i understand many hotels do not, in monterey and we were there when it was unusually hot. the beds were very comfortable and the room was clean. the staff we found to be very friendly, every one of them.  the free breakfast was what you would expect from a free breakfast bar not great but plenty of options and you will most likely find something you like.  we pretty much used the breakfast as a tide over until we were ready for brunch.  the location was great!  the location is really what you are paying for.  not only would i recommend to a friend, i already have.  our friends stayed at another hotel which had a larger room and was nicer looking from pictures but they were very unhappy,  to the point their room ended up being comped due to all the problems.  not to mention they spent over $40 in parking for the two days plus had to pay parking and wi-fi at the hotel.  we walked everywhere and the staff even allowed us to remain parked for a few hours, after check out. the only real downfall was the size of the room, if you plan on being in the room much, we were not.  oh and there was a little fridge, although we didn't use it, it was there :)

recent_review_text: small but clean and friendly. the room was a little on the small side but very clean.  it had air conditioning which was a huge plus, i understand many hotels do not, in monterey and we were there when it was unusually hot. the beds were very comfortable and the room was clean. the staff we found to be very friendly, every one of them.  the free breakfast was what you would expect from a free breakfast bar not great but plenty of options and you will most likely find something you like.  we pretty much used the breakfast as a tide over until we were ready for brunch.  the location was great!  the location is really what you are paying for.  not only would i recommend to a friend, i already have.  our friends stayed at another hotel which had a larger room and was nicer looking from pictures but they were very unhappy,  to the point their room ended up being comped due to all the problems.  not to mention they spent over $40 in parking for the two days plus had to pay parking and wi-fi at the hotel.  we walked everywhere and the staff even allowed us to remain parked for a few hours, after check out. the only real downfall was the size of the room, if you plan on being in the room much, we were not.  oh and there was a little fridge, although we didn't use it, it was there :)

recent_review_text: i stayed here overnight for a family trip to the aquarium. it was an enjoyable walking distance to our attraction along with the stores we visited along the way. the room we stayed in was meticulous and spacious for 2 adults, 2 teens, and one child. cvs is also nearby which was convenient for us when we needed to grab a few things. we just walked there and we walked there at around 10pm. it’s a quiet location—no dogs barking, didn’t hear people talking, and traffic sounds were very minimal. breakfast was also pretty good with variety of options. the service desk was considerably kind. i would highly recommend this place!

this broomfield resort provides complimentary wireless internet access, with a speed of 25+ mbps. business-friendly amenities include desks and phones; free local calls are provided (restrictions may apply). additionally, rooms include coffee/tea makers and irons/ironing boards. microwaves and hypo-allergenic bedding can be requested. a nightly turndown service is provided and housekeeping is offered daily.

. guests can play rounds at the 27-hole golf course. 2 outdoor swimming pools are on site along with a hot tub. other recreational amenities include a 24-hour fitness center.

the recreational activities listed below are available either on site or nearby; fees may apply.

recent_review_text:
i loved the pools and got tub.  the bar service was excellent with friendly servers who were efficient.  it made for a very relaxing day.

the breakfast buffet was also excellent.  the food was absolutely fresh & delicious.  it made for a great start to your day.  

you are close enough to denver or boulder without all the traffic and noise.

definitely recommend!!!

recent_review_text:
i chose to stay at hotel (camping) |MASK| at the recommendation of a friend, who has stayed there for years when working at pompeii. except for a few nights when i had to tolerate a loud neighbor, the room and grounds were quiet. the facilities were clean and in good repair, with excellent housekeeping and a staffed, friendly, and helpful front desk. i liked that the room had a mini-fridge, so i could keep some drinks, yogurt, fruit, and left-overs. the room also has a mini-safe to stash one’s travel docs and electronics.

people stay in the area for convenient access to the cirvumvesuviana and the main entrance of pompeii. unfortunately, most nearby restaurants close at 6:00 pm, so you need to stock your mini-fridge, walk into town, or up and around the corner to the few nearby restaurants for dinner.

my reservation came with breakfast every morning, which was very basic, continental: yogurt, espresso or cappuccino, cakes, croissants, ham or salami, and bread. the “juice” was really just a sweetened water drink. that said, the staff were very nice. for a more substantial breakfast, the bar/restaurant sgambati (owned by the same people?) serves omelettes, in addition to a regular menu of appetizers, salads, panini, pasta, and pizzas.

overall, i was very satisfied staying at hotel/camping |MASK| and look forward to returning
